name: stcrestclient

metadata.name: stcrestclient
metadata.summary: stcrestclient: Client modules for STC ReST API

documentation: See https://github.com/Spirent/py-stcrestclient#python-stc-rest-api-client-stcrestclient

modules.name: stcrestclient.adapt
documentation:
Install a new StcPython.py client module to enable use of STC ReST API.

This script replaces StcPython.py with new module that loads ReST API client
adapter module or legacy client module, depending on whether or not the
environment variable STC_REST_API is set to a non-empty value.  If there is an
existing legacy StcPython.py, it is renamed and will be used if STC_REST_API is
not set.

modules.name: stcrestclient.systeminfo
documentation:
Retrieve STC and API information from a system running a TestCenter server.

This module is provided as a convenient command line tool to get information
about a TestCenter server.

functions.name: stcrestclient.systeminfo.stc_system_info
documentation:
Return dictionary of STC and API information.

If a session already exists, then use it to get STC information and avoid
taking the time to start a new session.  A session is necessary to get
STC information.

modules.name: stcrestclient.stcpythonrest
documentation:
ReST API adapter for StcPython.py

The ReST API client adapter is functionally identical to the legacy
StcPython.py module, except that it communicates with the STC ReST API.
Without requiring any code changes, this allows STC automation scripts to
communicate over ReST, and not need a local STC installation.

This ReST adapter enables ReST API access if the environment variable
STC_REST_API is set to a non-empty value.  If STC_REST_API is not set, then the
non-rest STC client module is loaded if there is a local STC installation.

classes.name: stcrestclient.stcpythonrest.StcPythonRest

methods.name: stcrestclient.stcpythonrest.StcPythonRest.new_session
documentation:
Create a new session or attach to existing.

Normally, this function is called automatically, and gets its parameter
values from the environment.  It is provided as a public function for
cases when extra control over session creation is required in an
automation script that is adapted to use ReST.

WARNING:  This function is not part of the original StcPython.py and if
called directly by an automation script, then that script will not be
able to revert to using the non-ReST API until the call to this
function is removed.

Arguments:
server           -- STC server (Lab Server) address.  If not set get
                    value from STC_SERVER_ADDRESS environment variable.
session_name     -- Name part of session ID.  If not set get value from
                    STC_SESSION_NAME environment variable.
user_name        -- User portion of session ID.  If not set get name of
                    user this script is running as.
existing_session -- Behavior when session already exists.  Recognized
                    values are 'kill' and 'join'.  If not set get value
                    from EXISTING_SESSION environment variable.  If not
                    set to recognized value, raise exception if session
                    already exists.

See also: stchttp.StcHttp(), stchttp.new_session()

Return:
The internal StcHttp object that is used for this session.  This allows
the caller to perform additional interactions with the STC ReST API
beyond what the adapter provides.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stcpythonrest.StcPythonRest._check_session
documentation: Start a new session if one is not already started.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stcpythonrest.StcPythonRest._end_session
documentation: End the client session.
StcPythonRest is a substitute for StcPython that uses the STC ReST API.

modules.name: stcrestclient.stchttp
documentation:
STC REST API wrapper module.

This module allows python scripts to call the STC Automation API using HTTP.

To specify the HTTP (REST API) server, the client must set the value of the
environment variable STC_SERVER_ADDRESS, or specify the server when creating
the StcHttp object.

classes.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the REST API wrapper object.

If the port to connect to is not specified by the port argument, or by
the STC_SERVER_PORT environment variable, then try connecting on the
DEFAULT_PORT.

Arguments:
server      -- STC REST API server to connect to. None to use environ.
port        -- HTTP port to connect to server on.  Use environment
               variable STC_SERVER_PORT or DEFAULT_PORT if None.
api_version -- What API version to use.
debug_print -- Enable debug print statements.
timeout     -- Number of seconds to wait for a response.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.timeout
documentation: Return the current timeout value.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.set_timeout
documentation: Seconds to wait for a response.  Any zero-value means no timeout.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.new_session
documentation:
Create a new test session.

The test session is identified by the specified user_name and optional
session_name parameters.  If a session name is not specified, then the
server will create one.

Arguments:
user_name     -- User name part of session ID.
session_name  -- Session name part of session ID.
kill_existing -- If there is an existing session, with the same session
                 name and user name, then terminate it before creating
                 a new session
analytics     -- Optional boolean value to disable or enable analytics
                 for new session.  None will use setting configured on
                 server.

Return:
True if session started, False if session was already started.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.join_session
documentation: Attach to an existing session.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.end_session
documentation:
End this test session.

A session can be ended in three ways, depending on the value of the
end_tcsession parameter:
    - end_tcsession=None:
        Stop using session locally, do not contact server.
    - end_tcsession=False:
        End client controller, but leave test session on server.
    - end_tcsession=True:
        End client controller and terminate test session (default).
    - end_tcsession='kill':
        Forcefully terminate test session.

Specifying end_tcsession=False is useful to do before attaching an STC
GUI or legacy automation script, so that there are not multiple
controllers to interfere with each other.

When the session is ended, it is no longer available.  Clients should
export any result or log files, that they want to preserve, before the
session is ended.

Arguments
end_tcsession -- How to end the session (see above)
sid           -- ID of session to end.  None to use current session.
timeout       -- Seconds to wait for session to end.  None or 0 does
                 not wait and -1 waits forever for session to end.

Return:
True if session ended, false if session was not started.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.enable_debug_print
documentation: Enable debug messages.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.disable_debug_print
documentation: Disable debug messages.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.started
documentation: Return True is session is started.  Otherwise, return False.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.sessions
documentation:
Get a list of active sessions on the server.

Return:
List of session ID values, one for each active session on server.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.session_urls
documentation:
Get a list of active sessions on the server.

Return:
List of session URLs, one for each active session on server.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.session_info
documentation:
Get information on session.

If session_id is None, the default, then return information about this
session.  If a session ID is given, then get information about that
session.

Arguments:
session_id -- Id of session to get info for, if not this session.

Return:
Dictionary of session information.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.files
documentation: Get list of files, for this session, on server.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.file_urls
documentation:
Get list of files, for this session, on server.

Return:
List of session URLs, one for each file on server.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.bll_version
documentation:
Get the BLL version this session is connected to.

Return:
Version string if session started.  None if session not started.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.system_info
documentation: Return dictionary of STC and API information.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.apply
documentation: Send test configuration to chassis.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.get
documentation:
Returns the value(s) of one or more object attributes.

If multiple arguments, this method returns a dictionary of argument
names mapped to the value returned by each argument.

If a single argument is given, then the response is a list of values
for that argument.

Arguments:
handle -- Handle that identifies object to get info for.
*args  -- Zero or more attributes or relationships.

Return:
If multiple input arguments are given:
{attrib_name:attrib_val, attrib_name:attrib_val, ..}

If single input argument is given, then a single string value is
returned.  NOTE: If the string contains multiple substrings, then the
client will need to parse these.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.create
documentation:
Create a new automation object.

Arguments:
object_type -- Type of object to create.
under       -- Handle of the parent of the new object.
attributes  -- Dictionary of attributes (name-value pairs).
kwattrs     -- Optional keyword attributes (name=value pairs).

Return:
Handle of newly created object.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.createx
documentation:
Create a new automation object.

Arguments:
object_type -- Type of object to create.
under       -- Handle of the parent of the new object.
attributes  -- Dictionary of attributes (name-value pairs).
kwattrs     -- Optional keyword attributes (name=value pairs).

Return:
Dictionary containing handle of newly created object.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.delete
documentation:
Delete the specified object.

Arguments:
handle -- Handle of object to delete.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.perform
documentation:
Execute a command.

Arguments can be supplied either as a dictionary or as keyword
arguments.  Examples:
    stc.perform('LoadFromXml', {'filename':'config.xml'})
    stc.perform('LoadFromXml', filename='config.xml')

Arguments:
command -- Command to execute.
params  -- Optional.  Dictionary of parameters (name-value pairs).
kwargs  -- Optional keyword arguments (name=value pairs).

Return:
Data from command.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.config
documentation:
Sets or modifies one or more object attributes or relations.

Arguments can be supplied either as a dictionary or as keyword
arguments.  Examples:
    stc.config('port1', location='//10.1.2.3/1/1')
    stc.config('port2', {'location': '//10.1.2.3/1/2'})

Arguments:
handle     -- Handle of object to modify.
attributes -- Dictionary of attributes (name-value pairs).
kwattrs    -- Optional keyword attributes (name=value pairs).

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.chassis
documentation: Get list of chassis known to test session.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.chassis_info
documentation: Get information about the specified chassis.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.connections
documentation: Get list of connections.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.is_connected
documentation: Get Boolean connected status of the specified chassis.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.connect
documentation:
Establish connection to one or more chassis.

Arguments:
chassis_list -- List of chassis (IP addresses or DNS names)

Return:
List of chassis addresses.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.disconnect
documentation:
Remove connection with one or more chassis.

Arguments:
chassis_list -- List of chassis (IP addresses or DNS names)

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.connectall
documentation: Establish connections to all chassis (test ports) in this session.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.disconnectall
documentation: Remove connections to all chassis (test ports) in this session.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.help
documentation:
Get help information about Automation API.

The following values can be specified for the subject:
    None -- gets an overview of help.
    'commands' -- gets a list of API functions
    command name -- get info about the specified command.
    object type  -- get info about the specified object type
    handle value -- get info about the object type referred to

Arguments:
subject -- Optional.  Subject to get help on.
args    -- Optional.  Additional arguments for searching help.  These
           are used when the subject is 'list'.

Return:
String of help information.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.log
documentation:
Write a diagnostic message to a log file or to standard output.

Arguments:
level -- Severity level of entry.  One of: INFO, WARN, ERROR, FATAL.
msg   -- Message to write to log.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.download
documentation:
Download the specified file from the server.

Arguments:
file_name -- Name of file resource to save.
save_as   -- Optional path name to write file to.  If not specified,
             then file named by the last part of the resource path is
             downloaded to current directory.

Return: (save_path, bytes)
save_path -- Path where downloaded file was saved.
bytes     -- Bytes downloaded.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.download_all
documentation:
Download all available files.

Arguments:
dst_dir   -- Optional destination directory to write files to.  If not
             specified, then files are downloaded current directory.

Return:
Dictionary of {file_name: file_size, ..}

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.upload
documentation: Upload the specified file to the server.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.wait_until_complete
documentation:
Wait until sequencer is finished.

This method blocks your application until the sequencer has completed
its operation.  It returns once the sequencer has finished.

Arguments:
timeout -- Optional.  Seconds to wait for sequencer to finish.  If this
           time is exceeded, then an exception is raised.

Return:
Sequencer testState value.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.bulkconfig
documentation:
Sets or modifies one or more object attributes or relations.

Arguments can be supplied either as a dictionary or as keyword
arguments.  Examples:
    stc.bulkconfig('emulateddevice[@name="mydev"]/bgprouterconfig/bgpipv4routeconfig[0]',  {'NextHopIncrement': '0.0.1.0'})
    stc.bulkconfig('emulateddevice[@name="mydev"]/bgprouterconfig/bgpipv4routeconfig[1]',  NextHopIncrement='0.0.1.0')

Arguments:
locations     -- the locations of object to modify.
attributes -- Dictionary of attributes (name-value pairs).
kwattrs    -- Optional keyword attributes (name=value pairs).

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.bulkcreate
documentation:
Create a new automation object.

Arguments:
object_type -- Type of object to create.
attributes  -- Dictionary of attributes (name-value pairs).
kwattrs     -- Optional keyword attributes (name=value pairs).

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp._bulkcreateex
documentation:
Create a new automation object.

Arguments:
object_type -- Type of object to create.
under       -- Handle of the parent of the new object.
attributes  -- Dictionary of attributes (name-value pairs).
kwattrs     -- Optional keyword attributes (name=value pairs).

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.bulkget
documentation:
Returns the value(s) of one or more object attributes.

If multiple arguments, this method returns a dictionary of argument
names mapped to the value returned by each argument.

If a single argument is given, then the response is a list of values
for that argument.

Arguments:
handle -- Handle that identifies object to get info for.
args  -- Zero or more attributes or relationships.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.bulkperform
documentation:
Execute a command.

Arguments:
command -- Command to execute.
params  -- Optional.  Dictionary of parameters (name-value pairs).
kwargs  -- Optional keyword arguments (name=value pairs).

Return:
Data from command.

methods.name: stcrestclient.stchttp.StcHttp.bulkdelete
documentation:
bulkDelete the specified object.

Arguments:
handle -- Handles of objects to delete.
Spirent TestCenter ReST API wrapper object.

modules.name: stcrestclient.tccsh
documentation:
Spirent TestCenter Command Shell

Command shell that provides STC Automation API functionality using an
interactive command line interface, or command file.  This program accesses a
TestCenter Server over its HTTP interface, so no local BLL installation is
needed.

Type help to see help info.  Use <TAB> for command auto-completion.

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_ls
documentation:
List current sessions.
-l    List session info with each session.

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_info
documentation: Show information about the specified session: info testA - jdoe

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_delete
documentation:
Delete the specified session

Signals the session to cleanup and exit gracefully.

Synopsis:
    delete session_name

Example:
    delete traptest - waits

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_delete_all
documentation: Delete all sessions from server.

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_new
documentation:
Create and join a new test session.

The session that is created is named with the session name and the user
name separated with ' - '.

Synopsis:
    new [session_name] [user=user_name] [analytics=true/false]

Examples:
    new
    new test1
    new user=samuels
    new rfctest user=taylor
    new checknet user=recardo analytics=false

If the session name is not given, then a session name is automatically
assigned, starting with "AutoCreatedSession_00".  If the user name is
not specified, then tccsh tries to get the name of logged-in user.  If
analytics is not specified, then the current server setting is used.

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_files
documentation: List the files available in the current session.

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_join
documentation:
Join the specified session: join testA - jdoe

If no test session is specified, then the client stops using the
current session.  This does not communicate with the server, and will
not end the session on the server.

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_debug_on
documentation: Enable debug printing.

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_debug_off
documentation: Disable debug printing.

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_recording_on
documentation:
Enable recording commands to a file.

Commands recorded to a file can be executed later through tccsh.  This
is done by specifying the file, containing the recorded commands, using
the --file command line option.

When the recording feature is enabled, a '*' appears in the prompt.

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_recording_off
documentation: Stop recording commands.

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_exit
documentation: Exit the TestCenter command shell.

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_end
documentation:
End the current session: end yes

An optional 'yes' or 'no' argument specifies whether or not to end the
the server's test session (yes), or only end the controller (no).
To stop using the current session without affecting the server at all,
use the 'join' command without specifying a session.

If a yes or no argument is not provided, it is prompted for.

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_kill
documentation:
Terminate the specified test session

For STC ReST API version >= 2.30, this forcefully terminates the test
session on the server.  Otherwise, behavior is same as delete.

Synopsis:
    kill session_name

Example:
    kill so939 - joe

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_server
documentation:
Specify STC server.

If a port is not specified, then the value of the STC_SERVER_PORT
environment variable is used.  If that is not set, then the default
port 80 is tried.  If a connection cannot be make to the default, then
port 8888 is used.

Synopsis:
    server server_address [port]

Example:
    server 10.109.120.84 80

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_upload
documentation: Upload a file to the session: upload sample.tcc

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_download
documentation:
Download a file from the session.

Synopsis:
    download file [saveas=download_path]

Example:
    download bll.session.log
    download bll.session.log saveas=/tmp/sessionXYZ.log

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_download_all
documentation:
Download all available files.

Synopsis:
    download_all [save_dir]

Example:
    download_all
    download_all /tmp/testfiles

This command will not prompt before overwriting existing files.

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_system_info
documentation:
Shows information about the connected STC system.

The STC (BLL) version is only available if a current session is active.

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_set_timeout
documentation: Set number of seconds to wait for response.

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_stc_apply
documentation: Send test configuration to chassis.
Automation API

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_stc_get
documentation:
Returns the value(s) of one or more object attributes.

Synopsis:
    stc_get handle [attrib, ..]

Example:
    stc_get system1 version name

If multiple arguments, this method returns a dictionary of argument
names mapped to the value returned by each argument.

If a single argument is given, then the response is a list of values
for that argument.

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_stc_create
documentation:
Create a new automation object.

Attributes are specified as name=value.  If an attribute is assigned a
list of values, then the list of values must be quoted with each value
separated by a space: name="value1 value2 value3"

Synopsis:
    stc_create object_type [under] [attribute=value, ..]

Example:
    stc_create port project1
    stc_create port project1 location=//10.1.2.3/1/1

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_stc_perform
documentation:
Perform a command.

Parameters are specified as name=value.  If a parameter is assigned a
list of values, then the list of values must be quoted with each value
separated by a space: name="value1 value2 value3"

Synopsis:
    stc_perform command [param=value, ...]

Example:
    stc_perform SaveAsXml config=project1 filename=mytest.xml
    stc_perform AttachPorts portList="port1 port2 port3"

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_stc_config
documentation:
Sets or modifies one or more object attributes or relations.

Attributes are specified as name=value.  If an attribute is assigned a
list of values, then the list of values must be quoted with each value
separated by a space: name="value1 value2 value3"

Synopsis:
    stc_config handle [name=value, ...]

Example:
    stc_config port1 location=//10.100.20.60/1/1

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_stc_delete
documentation:
Delete the specified object.

Arguments:
handle -- Handle of object to delete.

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_chassis
documentation: Get list of chassis known by test session.

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_chassis_info
documentation:
Get information about the specified chassis.

Synopsis:
    chassis_info chassis_addr

Example:
    chassis_info 10.100.20.60

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_connections
documentation: Get the connected status of each chassis in the test session.

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_is_connected
documentation:
Get the connection status of the specified chassis.

Synopsis:
    is_connected chassis_addr

Example:
    is_connected 10.100.20.60

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_stc_connect
documentation:
Establish connection to one or more chassis.

Synopsis:
    stc_connect chassis_list

Example:
    stc_connect 10.100.20.60 10.100.20.61 10.100.20.62

Given a list of chassis (IP addresses or DNS names), connect the test
session to each chassis in list.

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_stc_connectall
documentation: Establish connections to all chassis (test ports) in this session.

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_stc_disconnect
documentation:
Disconnect one or more chassis

Synopsis:
    stc_disconnect chassis_list

Example:
    stc_disconnect 10.100.20.60 10.100.20.61 10.100.20.62

Given a list of chassis (IP addresses or DNS names), disconnect each
chassis in the list from the test session.

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_stc_disconnectall
documentation: Remove connections to all chassis (test ports) in this session.

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_stc_log
documentation:
Write a diagnostic message to a log file or to standard output.

Synopsis:
    stc_log level message

Example:
    stc_log ERROR Something unfortunate happened

The log level can be one of: INFO, WARN, ERROR, FATAL

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_stc_help
documentation:
Get help information about Automation API: help subject args

The following values can be specified for the subject:
    <empty>        -- gets an overview of help.
    commands       -- gets a list of API functions
    <command name> -- get info about the specified command.
    <object type>  -- get info about the specified object type
    <handle value> -- get info about the object type referred to
    list commands [wildcard]    -- get info about STC commands
    list configTypes [wildcard] -- get info about STC config types

List examples:
    stc_help list commands wait*
    stc_help list commands spirent.methodology.*

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell.do_wait_until_complete
documentation:
Wait until the sequencer is finished: wait_until_complete 30

Waits until the STC sequencer has completed its operation, or until the
timeout has elapsed.  If no timeout is specified, then waits forever.
Waiting can be interrupted with CTRL-C.

methods.name: stcrestclient.tccsh.TestCenterCommandShell._update_sessions
documentation: def do_EOF(self, s):
"""Exit the TestCenter command shell."""
return True
Utility methods

modules.name: stcrestclient.resthttp
documentation: Client class and exception for performing basic ReST API interactions.

classes.name: stcrestclient.resthttp.RestHttpError

methods.name: stcrestclient.resthttp.RestHttpError.__int__
documentation: Get HTTP status code.

methods.name: stcrestclient.resthttp.RestHttpError.__str__
documentation: Get error message.

methods.name: stcrestclient.resthttp.RestHttpError.status
documentation: Get HTTP status.
Exception object that returns error from remote call.

methods.name: stcrestclient.resthttp.ConnectionError.__int__
documentation: Get errno value.

methods.name: stcrestclient.resthttp.ConnectionError.__str__
documentation: Get error message.

methods.name: stcrestclient.resthttp.ConnectionError.__repr__
documentation: Get object representation string.

classes.name: stcrestclient.resthttp.RestHttp

methods.name: stcrestclient.resthttp.RestHttp.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the ReST API HTTP wrapper object.

Arguments:
base_url    -- Base URL for requests.  Ex: http://example.com/stuff/
user        -- Optional user name for basic auth.
password    -- Optional password for basic auth.
ssl_verify  -- Set to False to disable SSL verification (not secure).
debug_print -- Enable debug print statements.
timeout     -- Number of seconds to wait for a response.

methods.name: stcrestclient.resthttp.RestHttp.url
documentation: Construct a URL from the given components.

documentation: Return True if debug printing enabled.

documentation: Turn debug printing on.

methods.name: stcrestclient.resthttp.RestHttp.disable_debug_print
documentation: Turn debug printing off.

methods.name: stcrestclient.resthttp.RestHttp.timeout
documentation: Return the current timeout value.

methods.name: stcrestclient.resthttp.RestHttp.set_timeout
documentation: Seconds to wait for a response.  Any zero-value means no timeout.

methods.name: stcrestclient.resthttp.RestHttp.add_header

methods.name: stcrestclient.resthttp.RestHttp.download_file
documentation:
Download a file.

If a timeout defined, it is not a time limit on the entire download;
rather, an exception is raised if the server has not issued a response
for timeout seconds (more precisely, if no bytes have been received on
the underlying socket for timeout seconds). If no timeout is specified
explicitly, requests do not time out.

ReST API HTTP client wrapper object base class.

Derive application-specific ReST API class from this base class.
